questions.q: Who dragged Draupadi by her hair into the Kuru assembly?
options: Dushasana
Duryodhana
Karna
Shakuni
questions.explanation: Dushasana seized Draupadi by her hair and hauled her before the assembled kings.

questions.q: By whose grace, in the most beloved telling, did Draupadi's sari become endless?
options: Indra's
Shiva's
Krishna's
Surya's
questions.explanation: Draupadi called on Krishna and the cloth grew without end, though the critical edition describes the miracle without naming him.

questions.q: What question did Draupadi put to the assembled elders?
options: Why Krishna had not come to her aid
Whether dice played by trickery could bind anyone
Why Bhishma had not stopped the game
Whether a man who had lost himself could stake his wife
questions.explanation: Her unanswered question was whether Yudhishthira, having first gambled away his own freedom, had any right to stake her.

questions.q: Who commanded that Draupadi and the Pandavas be stripped of their garments?
options: Duryodhana
Karna
Shakuni
Dushasana
questions.explanation: It was Karna who ordered the clothes of the enslaved Pandavas and Draupadi taken, and Dushasana obeyed.

questions.q: What did Bhima vow to do to Dushasana for his cruelty?
options: Crush his skull with the mace
Tear open his chest and drink his blood
Drag him by the hair through the court
Cut off the hand that touched Draupadi
questions.explanation: Bhima swore before the assembly that he would rip open Dushasana's breast in battle and drink his blood.

questions.q: What did Bhima swear to break when Duryodhana insulted Draupadi?
options: His mace
His crown
His bow arm
His thigh
questions.explanation: Bhima vowed to shatter the thigh Duryodhana had bared, and he kept the vow in the final mace duel.

questions.q: Why did Duryodhana bare his thigh before Draupadi?
options: To display a battle scar
To mock Bhima's strength
As a lewd invitation for her to sit upon it
To show the mark of Gandhari's gaze
questions.explanation: Duryodhana slapped his bare thigh and beckoned Draupadi to sit on it, an obscenity that sealed his fate.

questions.q: Which frightened king, shaken by evil omens, finally intervened with boons?
options: Dhritarashtra
Bhishma
Drona
Shalya
questions.explanation: Terrified by the howling omens, Dhritarashtra rebuked Duryodhana and offered Draupadi boons to undo the harm.

questions.q: In what condition was Draupadi when she was dragged before the court?
options: Dressed in royal finery for the games
Asleep and unaware of the dice game
Clad in a single cloth, in her monthly courses
Veiled and surrounded by her attendants
questions.explanation: Draupadi was rajasvala, in her courses and wearing a single garment, which made the outrage all the greater.

questions.q: Which elder answered Draupadi only that the course of dharma is subtle?
options: Drona
Bhishma
Kripa
Bahlika
questions.explanation: Bhishma confessed that morality is subtle and could not say plainly whether Draupadi had been lawfully won.

questions.q: Which Kaurava alone stood up to argue that Draupadi had not been lawfully won?
options: Vikarna
Yuyutsu
Dushasana
Chitrasena
questions.explanation: Young Vikarna declared the stake invalid since Yudhishthira had lost himself first and had been goaded into staking her.

questions.q: How did Karna justify the treatment of Draupadi before the court?
options: She had laughed at Duryodhana's fall in Maya's hall
She had refused Karna at her swayamvara
A slave queen deserved no royal courtesies
A woman with five husbands was no chaste wife and could be treated so
questions.explanation: Karna branded a wife of five husbands a courtesan, declaring she could be brought to the hall clothed or not.

questions.q: Whom did Duryodhana first send to summon Draupadi to the hall?
options: His brother Dushasana
His charioteer Sanjaya
The gatekeeper of the sabha
The usher Pratikami
questions.explanation: Duryodhana first sent the suta usher Pratikami, and only after he returned empty-handed did Dushasana go.

questions.q: How did Draupadi respond to the first summons?
options: She sent back her question: did Yudhishthira lose himself first, or her?
She fled toward Kunti's apartments
She came at once, veiled in mourning white
She demanded Krishna be summoned as witness
questions.explanation: Refusing to come, Draupadi sent the usher back to ask the assembly whose master Yudhishthira was when he staked her.

questions.q: At Karna's order, what did the five Pandavas do in the hall?
options: They turned their faces from Draupadi
They cast off their upper garments as men who were won
They knelt before Duryodhana's throne
They broke their weapons and threw them down
questions.explanation: Accepting their status as staked and lost, the Pandavas silently removed their upper garments.

questions.q: What finally made Dushasana give up pulling at Draupadi's sari?
options: Bhima seized his wrists before the throne
Dhritarashtra commanded him to stop
He sank down weary before the endless heap of cloth
Vidura struck the staff of dharma upon the floor
questions.explanation: As garment after garment piled up in the hall, the exhausted Dushasana sat down in shame.

questions.q: Which omens terrified the court as Duryodhana mocked Draupadi?
options: An eclipse swallowed the midday sun
Jackals howled and asses brayed around the fire chamber
The palace doors burst from their hinges
A rain of blood fell upon the terrace
questions.explanation: Jackals howled in the homa chamber and asses and birds shrieked, omens that drove Dhritarashtra to intervene.

questions.q: Who joined Vidura in pressing the alarmed Dhritarashtra to act?
options: Queen Kunti
Sanjaya the charioteer
Queen Gandhari
The priest Dhaumya
questions.explanation: Gandhari, horrified by the omens, went with Vidura to the king and urged him to stop their son's outrage.

questions.q: Why was the seizing of Draupadi's hair a special desecration?
options: That hair had been sanctified by the waters of the Rajasuya rite
A queen's hair could be touched only by her maids
Her hair held the blessing of the fire she was born from
Loose hair doomed any man who touched it
questions.explanation: Draupadi lamented that locks blessed with the consecrated water of the Rajasuya were now clutched by Dushasana's fist.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana slyly invite Draupadi's own husbands to do?
options: To beg his pardon for her release
To wager her again in one more game
To carry her away if they dared
To declare themselves that Yudhishthira had no right to stake her
questions.explanation: Duryodhana offered to free Draupadi if Bhima and Arjuna would say their elder brother was not their master, a trap of dharma.

questions.q: How did Yudhishthira bear himself throughout Draupadi's ordeal?
options: He pleaded with Dhritarashtra for mercy
He wept aloud and tore his garments
He sat silent with downcast eyes
He recited the laws of dice aloud
questions.explanation: Bound by his own lost stake and his truth, Yudhishthira sat silent through the whole outrage.

questions.q: Beyond the disrobing, what further outrage did Karna pronounce upon Draupadi?
options: That she should choose another lord, for slaves could claim no marriage
That she must wash the feet of the Kaurava queens
That her sons would be raised as palace servants
That she must never again enter a royal hall
questions.explanation: Karna told Draupadi to enter the Kaurava household and select another husband, since a slave woman had no marriage to protect.

questions.q: In the oldest critical text of the epic, what preserves Draupadi's honor?
options: Krishna arrives in the hall and shields her
Indra rains down garments from heaven
Vyasa halts the assembly with a curse
The cloth multiplies as an unexplained marvel tied to dharma
questions.explanation: The critical edition simply describes garment after garment appearing as a wonder, while the vulgate and popular retellings credit Krishna's grace.

questions.q: What vow concerning her hair is Draupadi famed for in later tradition?
options: To shave her head until her husbands were freed
To leave it unbound till washed with Dushasana's blood
To veil her hair from every Kaurava eye
To braid it only after Duryodhana's death
questions.explanation: The vow to bind her hair only after washing it in Dushasana's blood is famous from the drama Venisamhara and later retellings rather than the critical epic.

questions.q: With what ancient tale did Vidura warn the court about leaving a question unanswered?
options: Yayati's fall from heaven
Nahusha's serpent curse
Shibi and the weighing of the dove
Prahlada's judgment between his son and Sudhanvan
questions.explanation: Vidura recalled how Prahlada answered truthfully even against his own son Virochana, warning that false or silent judges reap ruin.

questions.q: To whom did Bhishma finally say Draupadi's question must be referred?
options: To Vyasa as the voice of the gods
To Yudhishthira himself as the authority on dharma
To Dhritarashtra as master of the house
To the brahmins of the royal court
questions.explanation: Unable to resolve the subtlety, Bhishma said only Yudhishthira could declare whether she had been won.

questions.q: When did Bhima fulfill his blood vow against Dushasana?
options: On the war's seventeenth day
On the war's tenth day
On the war's fourteenth night
On the war's final day
questions.explanation: On the seventeenth day at Kurukshetra, Bhima tore open Dushasana's chest and drank his blood as sworn.

questions.q: Why was Draupadi's very presence in the sabha an outrage even before the disrobing?
options: A wife wedded by Vedic rites could not be summoned by ushers
Panchala custom forbade its queens from entering courts
Royal women like her had never been shown to a public assembly
A menstruating woman's entry laid a curse upon the hall
questions.explanation: Draupadi, whom even wind and sun had scarcely seen in her palace, was hauled before a hall of strangers, itself an unheard-of violation.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata does the disrobing of Draupadi occur?
options: The Adi Parva
The Sabha Parva
The Vana Parva
The Udyoga Parva
questions.explanation: The dice game and the assault on Draupadi fill the dice-game chapters of the Sabha Parva, the book of the assembly hall.

questions.q: By what Sanskrit name is the episode of the sari-pulling known?
options: Draupadi-shapatha
Sabha-pravesha
Keshagrahana
Vastraharana
questions.explanation: The episode is called the Vastraharana, the seizing of the garment, also rendered as Cheeraharana in many tellings.
